items.name: Leather Vest
items.description: A simple vest of boiled leather, stiff and cracked from years of use. It bears the scars of countless training bouts in the ludus. Every gladiator begins with one — few survive long enough to replace it.

items.name: Chain Mail
items.description: Interlocking iron rings forged by pit-smiths who never see the sun. The mail rattles softly with each step, a sound dreaded by those who hear it in the tunnels beneath the arena. Rust stains it like dried blood.

items.name: Pit Rags
items.description: Strips of filthy cloth wound tight around the torso, soaked in vinegar to harden them against blows. Slaves condemned to the arena wear these as their first and often last protection. They reek of desperation.

items.name: Hemp Gambeson
items.description: A padded tunic stuffed with coarse hemp fibers, stitched together by blind seamstresses in the arena cellars. It absorbs the shock of blunt weapons surprisingly well. The fabric is permanently stained dark with old sweat.

items.name: Bone Harness
items.description: A crude chest piece assembled from the rib bones of fallen beasts, lashed together with sinew. Arena shamans claim the bones remember pain and deflect it from the wearer. The clatter it makes is unsettling to opponents.

items.name: Iron Subarmalis
items.description: A Roman padded undergarment reinforced with thin iron strips sewn between layers of linen. Legionnaires discarded it as too heavy, but gladiators prize the extra protection. It chafes terribly against bare skin.

items.name: Tar-Coated Hide
items.description: Animal hide dipped in boiling tar until it hardened into a rigid shell. The black surface absorbs the heat of the arena sun, cooking its wearer slowly. Gladiators say it smells like the underworld itself.

items.name: Rust Lorica
items.description: A battered lorica segmentata salvaged from a legionnaire's corpse, its iron bands corroded to a deep orange. The hinges groan with every movement. It holds together through spite more than craftsmanship.

items.name: Gladiator Wrap
items.description: Thick linen bandages wound around the chest and abdomen in the style of retiarii fighters. Soaked in salt water and dried in the sun until stiff as wood. They offer little against steel but can save a life against teeth and claws.

items.name: Scrap Cuirass
items.description: Hammered together from broken shield fragments and bent nails by an imprisoned blacksmith. No two pieces match and the edges cut into the wearer's flesh. Ugly as sin, but it stops a blade well enough.

items.name: Bronze Plate
items.description: A gleaming breastplate of hammered bronze, shaped to mimic the musculature of the gods. It was once ceremonial armor for temple guardians before it found its way to the blood-soaked sands. The metal sings when struck.

items.name: Scorched Manica
items.description: Overlapping iron scales covering the torso, blackened by forge-fire until they resist corrosion. Originally crafted for beast-fighters who faced flame-spitting salamanders in the deep pits. The heat-marks form patterns like screaming faces.

items.name: Centurion's Remnant
items.description: The shattered remains of a centurion's lorica, reforged by arena smiths into a gladiator's cuirass. The original owner's name is still scratched into the inside. His ghost supposedly protects whoever wears it.

items.name: Wolf-Pelt Brigandine
items.description: Iron plates riveted between layers of black wolf hide, crafted by northern barbarians enslaved in the arena. The fur still carries the musk of the beast. Warriors claim they inherit the wolf's ferocity when donning it.

items.name: Ashen Guard
items.description: Armor forged from iron smelted with volcanic ash from Vesuvius, giving it a ghostly grey hue. The smiths who created it went blind from the fumes. It is unnervingly light for how much punishment it absorbs.

items.name: Spiked Lorica
items.description: A lorica hamata modified with iron spikes welded along the shoulders and chest. Opponents who grapple the wearer find themselves impaled. The spikes are filed from the bones of defeated champions.

items.name: Marshland Scale
items.description: Scale armor fashioned from the hides of swamp crocodiles, each scale naturally ridged to deflect slashing blows. It carries the damp smell of the marshes even in the driest arena. Wounds inflicted near it tend to fester.

items.name: Blood-Iron Plate
items.description: Iron quenched in bull's blood during the forging process, an ancient ritual said to bind the animal's vitality into the metal. The surface has a reddish tint that never fades. Gladiators swear it makes them harder to kill.

items.name: Tomb Warden's Mail
items.description: Chain mail recovered from a sealed Etruscan tomb, still gleaming despite centuries underground. The rings are impossibly fine and tightly woven. Those who wear it report hearing whispers of the dead during combat.

items.name: Gladius-Breaker Plate
items.description: A thick iron breastplate with hardened ridges designed to catch and snap sword blades on impact. Its creator was a secutor who grew tired of replacing shields. The ridges are filed with dark grooves from countless shattered weapons.

items.name: Shadow Guard
items.description: Armor dyed with the ink of deep-sea cuttlefish, so dark it seems to drink the light around it. Its wearer becomes difficult to track in the dim tunnels beneath the colosseum. Assassins and night-fighters covet it above all else.

items.name: Crimson Lorica
items.description: A masterwork lorica segmentata lacquered in deep crimson, the color of arterial blood. It was commissioned by a lanista who wanted his champion to be unmistakable on the sands. The dye is mixed with actual gladiator blood.

items.name: Wrath Pauldrons
items.description: Massive iron shoulder guards shaped like snarling daemon faces, extending down to cover the upper chest. They were forged during a lunar eclipse and quenched in the tears of condemned men. The faces seem to shift expression in torchlight.

items.name: Flayed King's Mail
items.description: Chain mail woven from rings of three different metals — iron, bronze, and blackened silver — in a pattern resembling flayed skin. A deposed barbarian king wore it to his execution in the arena and won his freedom instead. The crowd renamed him immortal.

items.name: Catacombs Cuirass
items.description: Steel breastplate recovered from the flooded catacombs beneath the arena, encrusted with mineral deposits that form eerie patterns. The metal has absorbed something from the depths — it is always cold to the touch. Blades seem to slide off its surface.

items.name: Minotaur Hide
items.description: Thick hide stripped from a labyrinth beast and cured in sulfur and ash until harder than boiled leather. The coarse brown surface still bears the creature's scars from a lifetime of rage. Wearing it fills the mind with blind fury.

items.name: Obsidian Scale Armor
items.description: Hundreds of razor-thin obsidian shards overlapping like dragon scales, bound to a leather backing with silver wire. It shatters spectacularly when struck hard, but each broken shard cuts anything that touches it. Arena crowds love the deadly glitter.

items.name: Iron Maiden Plate
items.description: A breastplate with short inward-facing spikes that press into the wearer's flesh, drawing blood with every movement. The pain keeps the gladiator alert and furious, and the blood feeds dark enchantments woven into the steel. Agony is its price; survival is its gift.

items.name: Phalanx Wall
items.description: An enormous curved chest plate modeled after the shields of a Greek phalanx, covering the wearer from collarbone to thigh. Movement is severely restricted, but frontal attacks are nearly useless against it. Champions of endurance favor it in drawn-out duels.

items.name: Venomcoat Brigandine
items.description: Leather brigandine soaked in basilisk venom and left to cure for a full moon cycle. The surface glistens with a faint green sheen that burns exposed skin on contact. Those who grapple its wearer learn this lesson once and never again.

items.name: Titan Shell
items.description: A massive suit of layered steel plates so thick that arrows bounce off harmlessly. Forged in the volcanic smithies of the deep earth by cyclops thralls. Its weight would crush a lesser warrior, but those strong enough to bear it become walking fortresses.

items.name: Hellfire Lorica
items.description: A lorica forged in flames drawn from a rift to the underworld, its iron bands glowing with faint embers that never cool. The heat cauterizes wounds almost as fast as they are dealt. Opponents hesitate to close the distance, fearing the searing air around it.

items.name: Gorgon Aegis
items.description: A breastplate embossed with the petrified face of a gorgon, its stone eyes still carrying a fraction of the creature's paralyzing gaze. Enemies who lock eyes with the face falter for a heartbeat. In the arena, a heartbeat is all it takes.

items.name: Reaper's Carapace
items.description: Black chitin harvested from a colossal beetle that burrowed up through the arena floor during a blood moon. The material is lighter than steel yet harder than bronze. Its surface is covered in fine grooves that channel blood away from the wearer.

items.name: Warlord's Mantle
items.description: A full torso covering of interlocking steel and dark iron, draped with a tattered war cloak stained by the blood of a hundred victories. It was stripped from a barbarian warlord who chose death over surrender. His rage still clings to the metal.

items.name: Colosseum Bulwark
items.description: Reinforced plate armor built from stones torn from the colosseum walls themselves, fused with iron through forbidden alchemy. It carries the weight of every death that occurred within those walls. The crowd roars louder when they see their arena made flesh.

items.name: Wraith Shroud
items.description: Armor woven from threads of shadow, harvested during the darkest hour of the winter solstice. It clings to the body like a second skin and seems to ripple with a will of its own. Blades sometimes pass through it as if cutting smoke.

items.name: Stygian Plate
items.description: Steel submerged in the waters of the River Styx by a priest of the death cults, turning it an impenetrable black. The metal remembers drowning and inflicts that memory on anyone who strikes it. Attackers describe sudden breathlessness and creeping dread.

items.name: Molten Core Guard
items.description: Plates of star-metal surrounding a core of perpetually molten ore, held in stasis by ancient runes. The armor radiates oppressive heat, and the air around it shimmers. If the runes ever fail, the wearer will be consumed in seconds.

items.name: Leviathan Scales
items.description: Scales pried from a sea leviathan that beached itself on the coast of Ostia, each one the size of a man's palm and harder than forged steel. They carry the deep-ocean cold and the creature's ancient malice. Salt water drips from them endlessly.

items.name: Dragonscale Aegis
items.description: Forged from the scales of a wyrm slain beneath Mount Etna, this armor shimmers with iridescent fire. Each scale was tempered in dragonblood and stitched together with enchanted sinew. It is said to make the wearer immune to fear itself.

items.name: Emperor's Aegis
items.description: The personal armor of a mad emperor who fought in the arena disguised as a common gladiator. It is gilded in cursed gold and lined with purple silk soaked in poison. Whoever wears it feels an insatiable hunger for adulation and slaughter.

items.name: Abyssal Fortress
items.description: Armor forged in the deepest chasm of Tartarus, where light has never reached. The plates are made of a metal that exists nowhere on earth, cold and impossibly dense. It weighs nothing to the damned but crushes the souls of the righteous.

items.name: Blessing of Mars
items.description: A suit of armor that materialized on the altar of Mars during a blood sacrifice, fully formed and radiating divine wrath. It bears no maker's mark and no smith can identify its alloy. Those who wear it enter a battle trance blessed by the god of war himself.

items.name: Veil of Mortuus
items.description: Not armor in any traditional sense but a shroud woven from the burial cloths of a hundred champions, animated by necromantic will. It wraps itself around the wearer protectively, hardening against strikes as if the dead themselves shield the living. It whispers names of the fallen between blows.

items.name: Obsidian Juggernaut
items.description: A towering suit of volcanic glass plates bound by magma-forged iron, enchanted to absorb the kinetic force of every blow. The accumulated energy makes the wearer stronger with each hit taken. Arena legends say it once survived a charging war elephant without a scratch.

items.name: Pluto's Embrace
items.description: Midnight-black plate armor that appears to absorb all surrounding light, leaving the wearer shrouded in unnatural darkness. It was offered as tribute by the lord of the underworld to his favorite mortal champion. The living who touch it feel their heartbeat slow.

items.name: Blood God's Mantle
items.description: Crimson plate armor that drinks the blood spilled upon it, mending its own cracks and strengthening its bonds. The more carnage surrounds the wearer, the more impervious it becomes. After great battles it pulses with a heartbeat of its own.

items.name: Primordial Carapace
items.description: Armor carved from the fossilized shell of a creature that predates the gods, unearthed during the excavation of a new arena pit. No tool can scratch it and no flame can heat it. Scholars believe it remembers the world before men and judges the wearer accordingly.

items.name: Eternal Lorica
items.description: A lorica that has existed since the founding of Rome, passed from champion to champion across the centuries. Each owner's name is etched into its surface in letters too small to read. It cannot be destroyed, cannot be lost, and always finds its way back to the arena.
